userStories.title: Bug Fix: Admin Image Changes Not Reflected on Public Menu Page
userStories.description: When admin uploads or changes an image for a menu item (e.g. MT04, MT01) via the admin panel, the change is not reflected on the public menu page at /en/menu. This is likely a caching issue — Next.js ISR cache or the image optimisation cache is serving the old image. Fix by: revalidating the menu page cache on image update, and ensuring the image URL includes a cache-busting mechanism.
acceptanceCriteria: After admin uploads a new image for any menu item, the public /en/menu page reflects the new image within 1 page reload (no waiting for ISR expiry)
The fix works for items like MT04 (Seafood Tom Yum) and MT01 (Pineapple Fried Rice)
No manual server restart required to see the update
Admin sees a confirmation that the image was saved and the page cache was cleared
Verify: upload new image for MT04 in admin → open /en/menu in a new tab → new image displayed
technicalNotes: Root cause is likely one of: (1) Next.js ISR cache (revalidate = 1800) not purged on image save, (2) next/image optimisation cache serving old image at the same URL, or (3) image file written to disk but menu data (photo field) not updated in DB
Fix 1 — revalidate on save: after PATCH /api/admin/menu/[id] with new image, call revalidatePath('/[locale]/menu', 'page') for all locales (en, ms, zh)
Fix 2 — cache-bust image URL: append ?v={updatedAt timestamp} to the image src so next/image treats it as a new URL: src={`/images/menu/${item.code}.jpg?v=${item.updatedAt}`}
Fix 3 — confirm DB update: ensure the image upload API (POST /api/admin/images or similar) actually updates a field in menu_items (e.g. photo_updated_at = NOW())
Check src/app/api/admin/images/route.ts — confirm it writes the file AND updates the DB
Check src/app/[locale]/menu/page.tsx — add revalidatePath call after any menu item update
Use Chrome DevTools Network tab to verify the image request URL and response headers (Cache-Control)
